ety: ćhulak˚ = Prk. छुलक्के(इ), fr. S. उद्+श्वल् ( = शल्)+कृ
def: v.n. To pass urine involuntarily (through fear or otherwise); to make water slowly (as a mare, &c. in heat); to discharge semen (cf. ćhulćhulānā).
v.t. To cause to touch, to make (one) touch or feel ( = ćhuwānā).
s. (dakh.) Gamesomeness, liveliness ( Harris ).
adj. Trifling, finical; boyish, childish, puerile; — weak in the genital organs or in the bladder, passing semen or urine involuntarily or quickly (through weakness, or fear, &c.).
more: adj. 1. Weak in the loins; discharging semen quickly. 2. Boyish; trifling; shallow.

def: Sant. v. n. 1. To drop; drip; trickle. 2. To itch; to titillate. 3. To feel the sexual passion.
v. n. To itch, to titillate.
more: v. n. To shriek, to scream, to screech.

def: n. m. chūlhī, chūhlī , n. f. S. चुल्लि, Pālī and Sah. chullī . A fireplace; hearth; oven. Chūlhe kī na chakkī kī . Wom. Not fit for the hearth, nor for the mill. (She can neither bake nor grind). Lāt mārī jhopṛī, salām bhāī chūhle . Azād. He kicked away his hut and bid his hearth adieu. (The practice of Azads who profess to have no ties with earth). chūlhā jhoknā , v. a. 1. To feed the fire. 2. To bake; cook. Sāre din chūlhā jhoktī hūṅ to roṭī miltī haí . Wom. chūlhe meṅ jhoknā, yā ḍālnā , v. a. To throw into the fire. Jab tap-i-furqat se jal-kar khāk ho-jaëṅge ham Āh! phir chūlhe meṅ ḍāleṅge terī tāsir ko? When in this feverish heat of absence I'm to ashes burned, What then avails my sighs? blast them—to ashes all be turned! chūlhe meṅ jānā, yā paṛnā , v. n. To be thrown into the fire; to go to pot. Mere or se chūlhe meṅ jāë! Wom. chūlhe niyot , An invitation to a feast given to every household for one inmate of each house.
adj. Lewd (Sant. bachkar ), libidinous (woman). Pat nāhīṅ nār chulharī kī . A lewd woman has no character.
(from چل chul ) adj. f. Lewd, libidinous (woman).
adj. Lascivious, lewd.
adj. Boyish, childish, puerile, silly ( = ćhulćhulā, ćhulohlā).
adj. (f. -ī), Trifling; childish, &c.( = ćhullū, ćhulćhulā, qq.v.). adj. Boyish, childish, puerile, silly ( = ćhulćhulā, ćhulohlā).
adj. (f. -ī) = ćhulohlā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Trifling; childish, &c.( = ćhullū, ćhulćhulā, qq.v.). adj. Boyish, childish, puerile, silly ( = ćhulćhulā, ćhulohlā).

n. f. A game similar to Hop, skip, and jump.
n. f. 1. A creaking sound produced by friction against any hard metal. 2. See ṭūṅ .
more: adv. Why; wherefore. chūṅ k., chūṅ charā k. , v. a. To why and wherefore; question; object. Chūṅ kare sāmne hamāre gair? Kab yeh uskī majāl hai, sāhib? Chirkīn.

ety: ćhū˚ = Prk. छुव(इ) = S. छुप(ति), rt. छुप्
def: s.m. Touching, touch, contact.
more: v. a. S. छुप् to touch; Pālī, chhupatī . 1. To touch; finger; thumb; feel; handle. Gagaryā morī chhū laī Turakvā ne, Rām! Bhar le jāūṅ to dharam jāt hai, Rīlī jāūṅ badnām . Song. Ṭhumrī . The Mussalman my pitcher touched, O Rām! My faith is gone if filled I take it home, If empty I return stained is my name. 2. To intermeddle; meddle with. chhūnā ; S. sparsh ; Sant. joṭet , n. m. Touch (A. lāmisah ); contact; contiguity chhūtī khaṛī lakīr ; A. umūd-i-mumās , ( Math .) chhūtī lakīr ; A. /?/khat-i-mumās , S. स्पर्शरेखा ( Math .) A tangent.
n. m. 1. The young of pigs. 2. A boy; son ( beṭā ).
v. a. To touch, to meddle with, to feel.
s. m. Young of any animal.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀙𑀼𑀯𑀇 (chuvaï). Cognate with Punjabi ਛੂਹਣਾ (chūhṇā) / چھوہنا (chūhṇā).

more: s. f. 1. Bodkin, case for needles. 2. A small cup for children.

def: n. f. 1. See chūṅ . 2. The chirping of birds. Sāṅj savere chiṛtyāṅ mil-kar chūṅchūṅ chūṅchūṅ kartī haiṅ, Chūṅchūṅ chūṅchūṅ chūṅchūṅ kyā woh 'Bechūṅ Bechūṅ' karte haiṅ .' Nazīr. 3. A squeaking toy ( chakhchakh ).

n. f. 1. A hag; a fury; harridan; beldam. 2. The ghost of a woman who died while pregnant. 3. A dirty or ugly old woman; a slut. 1. Hote the jāvānī meṅ to paryoṅ ke guzāre, Aur ab to chuṛail ā-ke bhī ek lāt na māre . Youth. Nazīr. In youth bright fairy forms would flutter round me keen, But now, grown old, no hell hag vile would kick me e'en. 2. Shakal chuṛail kī, mizāj paryon kā . Prov. As ogre ugly, as imperious as a fairy.
v.t. = ćhuṛānā, q.v.

subs: v.t. (intens.), To set free, &c. ( = ćhuṛānā); — to take or snatch away (from, -se), to rob (of).

n. f. H. chhū touch. Contamination; pollution; defilement. Hindū baṛī chhūt karte haiṅ. chhūt h. , v. n. To be touched, polluted.

n. f. S. छोरण leaving. 1. Separation; disjunction; detachment; severance; divorce. Chūṭ chuṭāo kar dīyā . 2. Relinquishment; leisure ( chuṭkārā ). Mujhe apne kām se chhūṭ kahāṅ! 3. A drawback; exemption; remission of revenue; reduction in the assessment. 4. An exception; rejection ( chhuṭ ). 5. A catch-word. 6. Leavings ( jhūṭ , 1). 7. ( choṭ, choṛ ) The grain left on the threshing floor and swept up by the chamār . 8. Full liberty or licence. Achchhī chhūṭ huī . 9. A bet ( hoṛ ). Āo, das das rupaë kī chhūṭ! 10. Letting pigeons loose on a bet that they return to their cot. 11. Striking off-hand or playing loose in fencing. 12. See bel , 10. chhūṭ , p. p. Excepted; excluded; rejected; separate. chū'bol ; A. harf-i-tardīd , ( Gram .) A disjunctive particle. chhūṭ chhuṭāo , n. m. See chhūṭ , 1. and bīch bichāo , 1. chhūṭ laṛnā , v. n. 1. To hit all round. 2. To let loose one's tongue.
subs: v.n. To be touched, be contaminated, be defiled, be polluted.

def: adj. Little, small, &c. (used in comp.)
subs: s.m. Poor, low, or common people

def: prep. (governing the formative), Omitting, excepting, save, but, without (e.g. tujh ćhuṭ, 'excepting thee'; — syn. ćhoṛ).

s.f. Severance, separation; divorce; liberation, deliverance, release; leaving; relinquishment; freedom, liberty; escape; exemption (from, -se); remission (of revenue, &c.); exception; rejection; leavings, refuse; grain left on the threshing floor; — radiance or splendour (of jewels, &c.); reflection (of a mirror, &c.); — a catch-word; — (in fencing) striking off hand or playing loose, striking the opponent's body anywhere one pleases; — giving money off hand or at will to jugglers, dancing girls, &c., at entertainments; — a bet; letting pigeons fly on a bet that they return to their cot; — adv. Omitting, excepting; apart, aside, &c. ( = ćhuṭ, q.v.)
more: s. f. 1. Remission, leaving. 2. Striking off hand or playing loose in fencing. 3. Radiance or splendour of jewels, reflection of a mirror, &c. 4. Giving money off hand or at will to jugglers, &c. at entertainments.
subs: s.m. Severance, separation, parting; intervention, mediation
v.n. To fight loosely, to hit all round; — to let (the tongue) loose.
wety: From the stem of چُھوٹْنَا (chūṭnā, “to be released”).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Touching
more: adj. S. क्षुद्र 1. Small; little; less. 1. Deh baṛhe aur ghaṭe, deh hotī dublī moṭī, Ruhe ātmā jūṅ kī t/?/ṅ, nahīṅ hove baṛī chhoṭī . Bhajan. The body grows now big, now small, or lean or stout, The soul the same remains, nor lessens nor swells out. 2. Chhoṭā muṅh baṛi bāt . Riddle. Ans. Top . A gun. Small mouth, big words (acts). 2. Younger; junior. 1. Chhoṭā so khotā . Prov. Small stature, bad nature. 2. Chhoṭā so moṭā . Prov. Short and fat. 3. Common; low; mean; weak; paltry; contemptible. Dushman ko chhoṭā na jāno! Never despise an enemy. 4. Narrow; niggard; low-minded. Chhoṭe dil kā ādmī hai . chhoṭe ādmī , n. m. Poor or common men; persons of no distinction; private individuals. chhoṭā adh-kaṭ , (Math.) The minor axis. chhoṭā afsar , n. m. A subordinate officer. chhoṭā bāṅṭne-vālā ; A. zūazāf-i-agl , ( Math .) The least common multiple; L. C. M. chhoṭā bhāī , n. m. A younger brothe…

s.f. Littleness, smallness ( = ćhoṭāʼī, q.v.). s.f. = H چهوٿاپن छोटापन ćhoṭā-pan, s.m. See ćhuṭāpā, ćhuṭpan and ćhuṭāʼī.
adj. = ćhuṭkā; — adv. = ćhuṭ and ćhūṭ, qq.v. adj. (f. -ī), Little, small, diminutive; lesser; younger, junior; — the little or lesser one; the younger one.
v. n. 1. To nip; bite. Sāṅp ne chuṭak līyā . 2. To pluck (a flower).
more: n. m. S. चपेट A slap on the face; a box on the ear.

v. n. S. चट to break. 1. To crackle; crack; burst. 2. To snap; splinter; shiver. 3. To be off like a shot; to depart suddenly. 4. To open out; bloom (a flower). Gulāb chaṭakhne lagā . 5. To be nettled or irritated; to flare up. Bābā chata/?/khtā kyoṅ hai? Lāl rah! Āzād. 6. To speak rudely or saucily. Parāyā den aisā hī hotā hai, jab māṅgo jab chaṭakh-kar boltī hai . It's always so with those you lend money to, whenever you ask for it you only get angry words. 7. To be alienated, estranged. 8. To fall out; break with; to be cuts. chaṭkho ke havāle , intj. Away; be off; out upon you!

n. m. S. चटु pleasing discourse, कला art. 1. A. ( latīfah ) Bon mot ; pun; witticism. 2. Pleasantry; humour; facetiousness; banter. 3. A squib; a mischievous joke. 4. A specific cure; antidote. chuṭkulā chhoṛnā , v. n. To invent a playfully mischievous story. Roz ek chuṭkulā chhoṛ jātā hai .

ety: ćhuṭ˚ = Prk. छुट्ट(इ) = S. छुट्य(ते), pass. of rt. छुट्
def: v.n. To be set free, be liberated, be discharged, be acquitted; to be redeemed (a pledge); to be let go; to get loose; to be loose; to be dishevelled (the hair); to escape (from); to slip (from); to be adrift; to be got rid of, to be separated (from); to be left, be abandoned; to be left out, be omitted; to be left off, be given up, be relinquished; to be let off, be discharged, to go off (a gun); to be free with (the hands), use (the hands) freely (in striking); to cease, stop; to issue, to come out or forth (as blood, life, &c.), to spout, to shoot forth; to come out or off (as colour from cloth, &c.); to begin to move, be set in motion (as sand, or a train, &c.), to start

Compare چُھوٹْنَا (chūṭnā, “to be released”) and چُھوٹ (chūṭ, “liberty, freedom”). Cognate with Punjabi ਛੁੱਟ (chuṭṭa) / چُھٹّ (chuṭṭ), Gujarati છુટ્ટું (chuṭṭũ), Marathi सुटे (suṭe).

more: n. f. S. चिल्ल A kite (Sant. kuṛit ); Falco chīlā . 1. Kālī chīl, Maṅgal kā roz! Street cry. A pice to let the kite go on Tuesday! ☞ Fowlers trade on the superstitious piety which purchases for a pice the liberty of a kite caught for the express purpose of being whirled round the head of a child on a Tuesday or Saturday and then let go. 2. Hote sīrat se haiṅ mardān-i-dilāvar mumtāz, Warnah sūrat meṅ to kuchh kam nahīṅ shahbāz se chīl . Zauq. It's heart distinguishes the generous and bold, In outward looks the hawk and kite the same behold. 3. Chīl ke ghar meṅ mās kahāṅ? Prov. No flesh in a vulture's nest. (No money in a Mahommedan's house). (He is such a spendthrift!) 4. Māl ho āshiqon ke pās kahāṅ? Chīl ke ghoṅsle meṅ mās kahāṅ? No lover can keep property; with kites no store of flesh you 'll see. 5. Chīl ke ghar meṅ pāras hotā hai . Prov. The philosopher's stone is in the kite's nest. …
s. f. A kite. (Falco cheela: Lath. ) چيل جهپٿا مارنا يا کرنا chīl jhapāṭṭā mārnā or karnā, To snatch.

Riddle. Ans. The hole of a chilam . New loose, old tight. chilam-bardār , n. m. A servant who prepares the chilam , q. v. chilam-bardārī , n. f. Menial or low service. chilam tambākū , n. m. A little tobacco, enough for one chilam. chilam-chaṭ , n. m. One addicted to smoking in excess. chilmeṅ bhar'nā , v. a. 1. To prepare the chilam ; to fill a pipe. 2. To serve or wait upon one. Kyā tāb dil jaloṅ se jo barq lāg rakkhe, Doza/?/kh bhī ho to inkī chilmoṅ par āg rakkhe . Zauq.
